# Targets

<!-- generated by tools/sourcing/targets_board.py вҖ” do not edit by hand -->

The unsorry worklist: theorems that are **already proven but not yet in mathlib**, vetted for absence and stated in Lean, waiting for an agent or a human to prove them. Claim one, open a PR, let the gates decide (see [Running an agent](../README.md#running-an-agent) and [ADR-012](adrs/ADR-012-Backlog-Sourcing.md)).

| `abstract-regular-polyhedron-classification` вҖ” For an abstract regular polyhedron вҖ” V vertices, E edges, F faces that are p-gons, vertices of degree q вҖ” with the two handshakes pВ·F = 2E and qВ·V = 2E and Euler's relation V + F = E + 2, the pair (p, q) is one of the five Platonic SchlГӨfli pairs {(3,3),(3,4),(4,3),(3,5),(5,3)}. The classification (вҹ№) half of Freek #50 in combinatorial/Euler form. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | Freek 100 (#50), combinatorial form (ADR-031 / SPEC-031-A, Track 1) | The classification half of 'there are exactly five Platonic solids', reusing the proved `platonic_schlafli_pairs` as keystone (Euler + handshake вҹ№ 1/p+1/q > 1/2 вҹ№ the five pairs). Coxeter, Regular Polytopes, Ch. 1. NOT the geometric Freek #50 (that is Track 2, gated on a mathlib polytope face lattice + EulerвҖ“PoincarГ©). |
| `abstract-regular-polyhedron-realizable-iff` вҖ” The Track-1 **existence-biconditional**: for p, q вүҘ 3, the pair (p,q) is a Platonic SchlГӨfli pair {(3,3),(3,4),(4,3),(3,5),(5,3)} **iff** it is realizable by an abstract regular polyhedron (вҲғ V E F > 0 with pВ·F=2E, qВ·V=2E, V+F=E+2). | proved | 4 | вҖ” | The capstone of Freek #50's combinatorial classification (ADR-031, Track 1) вҖ” the labelled combinatorial/Euler form, explicitly NOT the geometric #50. | вҹ№ is the existence direction (`platonic-pairs-realizable`); вҹё is the proved classification (`abstract-regular-polyhedron-classification`). Composing them gives the biconditional. mathlib has neither. |
| `alt-geometric-ratio-eight` вҖ” An alternating geometric series closed form (ratio -8): (8+1) times the sum over k in range n of (-8)^k equals 1 - (-8)^n. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | self-seeded alternating-geometric identity family. | geometric telescoping with ratio -r; proved by induction on n. |

| `geud-30-pow-69-sub-self` вҖ” 30 (= 2 * 3 * 5) divides n to the 69 minus n, for every integer n. Here 30 is the EXACT maximal universal divisor P(69): the product of all primes p with (p-1) dividing 68. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | self-seeded polynomial-divisibility identity family. | 30 (= 2 * 3 * 5) divides n to the 69 minus n, for every integer n. Here 30 is the EXACT maximal universal divisor P(69): the product of all primes p with (p-1) dividing 68. Provable by exhibiting the cofactor and `ring`. |

| `nat-add-assoc-thm` вҖ” Addition of natural numbers is associative. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-add-comm-thm` вҖ” Addition of natural numbers is commutative. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-add-left-cancel` вҖ” Left addition is cancellative on the naturals. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-add-zero-thm` вҖ” Zero is a right identity for natural-number addition. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-dvd-refl` вҖ” Divisibility is reflexive on the naturals. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-even-or-odd` вҖ” Every natural number is even or odd. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-gcd-comm` вҖ” The gcd of two naturals is symmetric. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-le-add-right` вҖ” A natural number is at most itself plus another. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-le-succ` вҖ” Every natural number is at most its successor. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-mul-add-distrib` вҖ” Multiplication distributes over addition on the left for naturals. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-mul-comm-thm` вҖ” Multiplication of natural numbers is commutative. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-mul-one-thm` вҖ” One is a right identity for natural-number multiplication. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |
| `nat-mul-zero-thm` вҖ” Any natural number times zero is zero. | proved | 1 | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” |

| `nicomachus-sum-cubes-eq-sum-id-sq` вҖ” The sum of the first n cubes equals the square of the sum of the first n naturals (Nicomachus's theorem). | proved | 3 | вҖ” | #400 Identity Engine (ADR-043) вҖ” algebraic identity family; promoted from candidate backlog (#610). | The sum of the first n cubes equals the square of the sum of the first n naturals (Nicomachus's theorem). Not a named mathlib lemma in this form. |
| `nicomachus-sum-cubes-triangular` вҖ” For every natural n, the sum over i in 0..n of i^3 equals (n(n+1)/2)^2 (the explicit triangular-number form of Nicomachus' theorem). | proved | 3 | [packet-ready](upstream/nicomachus-sum-cubes-triangular.md) | Freek 100 / classic | Nicomachus of Gerasa, Introduction to Arithmetic, Book II, in explicit closed form; Graham, Knuth & Patashnik, Concrete Mathematics, В§2.5 (вҲ‘k^3 = (n(n+1)/2)^2). |
| `ninth-power-mod-nineteen-mem` вҖ” Every ninth power is congruent to only 0, 1 or 18 modulo the prime 19. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | #400 Identity Engine (ADR-043) вҖ” power-residue family; promoted from candidate backlog (#610). | Every ninth power is congruent to only 0, 1 or 18 modulo the prime 19. Not a named mathlib lemma in this form. |
| `no-int-sq-eq-eight-mul-add-three` вҖ” No integer square has the form 8n+3 (squares are 0,1,4 mod 8). | proved | 3 | вҖ” | Classic elementary number theory (library-growth batch, #400 plan Phase 3). | No integer square has the form 8n+3 (squares are 0,1,4 mod 8). mathlib has `ZMod.pow_card` (Fermat) but not these specific named divisibility lemmas. |
| `no-nat-sq-eq-two-mul-sq` вҖ” There are no positive naturals a, b with aВІ = 2В·bВІ вҖ” В¬вҲғ a b, 0 < b вҲ§ aВІ = 2bВІ: the irrationality of вҲҡ2 in elementary infinite-descent form. | proved | 4 | вҖ” | Freek 100 (#1, irrationality of вҲҡ2), infinite-descent / parity form | Classic infinite descent: aВІ=2bВІ вҹ№ a even вҹ№ b even вҹ№ a strictly smaller solution. mathlib proves `irrational_sqrt_two` over в„қ via prime factorisation; this self-contained в„• descent statement (no reals, no `sqrt`) is absent and is the elementary heart of Freek #1. |

| `not-prime-pow-four-add-four` вҖ” For every natural n with n > 1, n^4 + 4 is not prime, via the Sophie Germain factorization n^4+4 = (n^2-2n+2)(n^2+2n+2). | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities | Sophie Germain's identity, compositeness corollary. SierpiЕ„ski, Elementary Theory of Numbers (PWN/North-Holland, 1988); standard olympiad result. |
| `numderangements-add-two-int-form` вҖ” The derangement count of an (n+2)-set is (n+1) times the sum of the derangement counts of the (n+1)- and n-element sets, stated over the integers. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | #400 Identity Engine (ADR-043) вҖ” partition/generating-function family; promoted from candidate backlog. | The derangement count of an (n+2)-set is (n+1) times the sum of the derangement counts of the (n+1)- and n-element sets, stated over the integers. Not a named mathlib lemma in this form. |
| `odd-fourth-power-mod-sixteen` вҖ” The fourth power of every odd natural number leaves remainder 1 on division by 16: if n is odd then nвҒҙ % 16 = 1. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities (fourth-power congruence tower вҖ” leaf; compounds on `odd-sq-mod-eight`) | Standard elementary number theory: odd squares are вүЎ 1 (mod 8), so odd fourth powers are вүЎ 1 (mod 16). Hardy & Wright, An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers (quadratic-residue preliminaries). One power up from the proved `odd-sq-mod-eight`. |
| `odd-sq-mod-eight` вҖ” The square of every odd natural number leaves remainder 1 on division by 8: if n is odd then n^2 % 8 = 1. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities | Odd squares are вүЎ 1 (mod 8); Hardy & Wright, An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers (quadratic-residue preliminaries); standard elementary number theory. |
| `odd-square-sum-coeff-eight` вҖ” A scaled odd-square-sum closed form (coefficient 8): three times the sum over k in range n of 8В·(2k+1)ВІ equals 8В·nВ·(2nвҲ’1)В·(2n+1). | proved | 1 | вҖ” | self-seeded odd-square-sum identity family. | sum of the first n odd squares; proved by induction on n. |

| `one-add-four-b-fourth-not-prime` вҖ” For every natural b > 1, 1 + 4bвҒҙ is not prime; by the Sophie Germain factorization 1 + 4bвҒҙ = (2bВІ+2b+1)(2bВІвҲ’2b+1), with both factors exceeding 1. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities (compositeness-via-factorization вҖ” the Sophie Germain a=1 corollary) | The a=1 case of Sophie Germain's identity aвҒҙ+4bвҒҙ = (aВІвҲ’2ab+2bВІ)(aВІ+2ab+2bВІ); a companion to the proved `not-prime-pow-four-add-four` (which is the b=1 case nвҒҙ+4). SierpiЕ„ski, Elementary Theory of Numbers. |

| `platonic-pairs-realizable` вҖ” Each of the five Platonic SchlГӨfli pairs {(3,3),(3,4),(4,3),(3,5),(5,3)} is **realizable** by an abstract regular polyhedron: there exist V, E, F (>0) satisfying the two handshakes pВ·F=2E, qВ·V=2E and Euler V+F=E+2. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | The **existence (вҹё) direction** of Freek #50's combinatorial classification (ADR-031, Track 1). | `pow-four-add-sq-add-one-factor` вҖ” For every integer n, nвҒҙ + nВІ + 1 = (nВІ + n + 1)(nВІ вҲ’ n + 1); the classic Aurifeuillian-style factorization of xвҒҙ+xВІ+1 (a product of the two "cyclotomic-like" quadratics). | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities (compositeness-via-factorization вҖ” the factorization leaf) | Standard algebra: xвҒҙ+xВІ+1 = (xВІ+1)ВІвҲ’xВІ = (xВІ+x+1)(xВІвҲ’x+1). | `pow-four-add-sq-add-one-not-prime` вҖ” For every natural n > 1, nвҒҙ + nВІ + 1 is not prime; it factors as (nВІ+n+1)(nВІвҲ’n+1) with both factors exceeding 1. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities (compositeness-via-factorization вҖ” the **capstone**; compounds on `pow-four-add-sq-add-one-factor`) | Classic olympiad compositeness result, the xвҒҙ+xВІ+1 analogue of the proved `not-prime-pow-four-add-four` (Sophie Germain). | `prime-fourth-power-mod-240` вҖ” The fourth power of every prime p > 5 leaves remainder 1 on division by 240: if p is prime and p > 5 then pвҒҙ % 240 = 1. | proved | 4 | вҖ” | classic identities (fourth-power congruence tower вҖ” **root**; deps: the mod-16, mod-3, mod-5 leaves) | Classic competition gem "240 вҲЈ pвҒҙ вҲ’ 1 for every prime p > 5", one power up from the proved-here "24 вҲЈ pВІ вҲ’ 1 for primes p > 3" (binto-labs, `prime-sq-mod-twenty-four`). 240 = 16В·3В·5; the result is CRT over the three coprime moduli. | `sum-range-pow-five-add-pow-seven` вҖ” For every natural n, (sum of iвҒө for i in 0..n) + (sum of iвҒ· for i in 0..n) = 2В·(sum of i for i in 0..n)вҒҙ; i.e. вҲ‘kвҒө + вҲ‘kвҒ· = 2(вҲ‘k)вҒҙ = 2TвҒҙ where T = n(n+1)/2 is the n-th triangular number. | proved | 4 | вҖ” | classic identities (power-sum tower вҖ” the **crown**: compounds on `sum-range-pow-five-closed-form` + `sum-range-pow-seven-closed-form`) | A classic Faulhaber curiosity: the sum of the fifth- and seventh-power sums is exactly twice the fourth power of the triangular number, generalising Nicomachus's вҲ‘kВі = (вҲ‘k)ВІ = TВІ one octave up. Verified вҲҖ n; see Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993) for the triangular-number structure of odd-power sums. |
| `sum-range-pow-five-closed-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 12 * (sum of i^5 for i in 0..n) = n^2 (n+1)^2 (2n^2 + 2n - 1); Faulhaber's closed form for fifth powers, вҲ‘k^5 = (2n^6 + 6n^5 + 5n^4 - n^2)/12. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities | Faulhaber's formula, p = 5 case; Conway & Guy, The Book of Numbers (sums of powers); D. E. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables. |
| `sum-range-pow-five-faulhaber-triangular` вҖ” For every natural n, 3В·(sum of iвҒө for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)ВІВ·(4В·(sum of i for i in 0..n)вҲ’1); i.e. вҲ‘kвҒө = TВІ(4TвҲ’1)/3 where T = вҲ‘k. Faulhaber's theorem made concrete: the fifth-power sum is a pure polynomial in the triangular number T. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities (Faulhaber-in-T tower вҖ” odd-power rung; compounds on `sum-range-pow-five-closed-form`) | Faulhaber's 1631 result that odd-power sums are polynomials in T = n(n+1)/2: вҲ‘kвҒө = (4TВівҲ’TВІ)/3 = TВІ(4TвҲ’1)/3. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993). |
| `sum-range-pow-four-closed-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 30 * (sum of k^4 for k in 0..n) = n(n+1)(2n+1)(3n^2+3n-1), the integer (в„Ө) form of the Faulhaber p=4 identity. | proved | 2 | [packet-ready](upstream/sum-range-pow-four-closed-form.md) | classic identities | Faulhaber's formula, case p=4. | `sum-range-pow-four-triangular-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 15В·(sum of iвҒҙ for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)В·(2n+1)В·(3nВІ+3nвҲ’1); i.e. вҲ‘kвҒҙ = T(2n+1)(3nВІ+3nвҲ’1)/15 where T = вҲ‘k. | proved | 3 | вҖ” | classic identities (Faulhaber-in-T tower вҖ” even-power rung) | Faulhaber's theorem, even-power case: вҲ‘kвҒҙ = n(n+1)(2n+1)(3nВІ+3nвҲ’1)/30 = T(2n+1)(3nВІ+3nвҲ’1)/15. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993); CRC Standard Mathematical Tables. |
| `sum-range-pow-seven-closed-form` вҖ” For every natural n, 24В·(sum of iвҒ· for i in 0..n) = nВІ(n+1)ВІ(3nвҒҙ+6nВівҲ’nВІвҲ’4n+2); Faulhaber's closed form for seventh powers, вҲ‘kвҒ· = (3nвҒё+12nвҒ·+14nвҒ¶вҲ’7nвҒҙ+2nВІ)/24. | proved | 4 | вҖ” | classic identities (power-sum tower вҖ” the harder odd-power rung) | Faulhaber's formula, p = 7; Conway & Guy, The Book of Numbers; D. | `sum-range-pow-seven-faulhaber-triangular` вҖ” For every natural n, 3В·(sum of iвҒ· for i in 0..n) = (sum of i for i in 0..n)ВІВ·(6В·(sum of i for i in 0..n)ВІвҲ’4В·(sum of i for i in 0..n)+1); i.e. вҲ‘kвҒ· = TВІ(6TВІвҲ’4T+1)/3 where T = вҲ‘k. Knuth, "Johann Faulhaber and sums of powers", Math. Comp. 61 (1993). |

| `thirty-dvd-pow-five-sub-self` вҖ” For every integer n, 30 вҲЈ nвҒө вҲ’ n (Fermat: 2,3,5 each divide nвҒөвҲ’n). | proved | 2 | вҖ” | Classic elementary number theory (library-growth batch, #400 plan Phase 3). | For every integer n, 30 вҲЈ nвҒө вҲ’ n (Fermat: 2,3,5 each divide nвҒөвҲ’n). mathlib has `ZMod.pow_card` (Fermat) but not these specific named divisibility lemmas. |
| `three-cubes-div-nine` вҖ” For every natural n, 9 divides n^3 + (n+1)^3 + (n+2)^3; the sum of any three consecutive cubes is divisible by 9. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | classic identities | Classic introductory number-theory / olympiad exercise; Engel, Problem-Solving Strategies (divisibility chapter); SierpiЕ„ski, Elementary Theory of Numbers (PWN/North-Holland, 1988). |
| `three-cubes-minus-three-prod-dvd-sum` вҖ” The sum a+b+c divides the symmetric expression a cubed plus b cubed plus c cubed minus three times abc. | proved | 2 | вҖ” | #400 Identity Engine (ADR-043) вҖ” algebraic identity family; promoted from candidate backlog (#610). | The sum a+b+c divides the symmetric expression a cubed plus b cubed plus c cubed minus three times abc. Not a named mathlib lemma in this form. |

| `weighted-am-gm-cubed` вҖ” For nonneg reals x,y, 2xВі+yВі вүҘ 3xВІy вҖ” a weighted AMвҖ“GM, since 2xВі+yВівҲ’3xВІy = (xвҲ’y)ВІ(2x+y). | proved | 2 | вҖ” | Classic real inequality (library-growth batch, #400 plan Phase 3). The project had almost no inequalities; this seeds the SOS/nlinarith family. | For nonneg reals x,y, 2xВі+yВі вүҘ 3xВІy вҖ” a weighted AMвҖ“GM, since 2xВі+yВівҲ’3xВІy = (xвҲ’y)ВІ(2x+y). mathlib has the abstract CauchyвҖ“Schwarz / power-mean lemmas but not this concrete polynomial form as a named lemma. |
